seoTitle: How to Fix Outlook Not Receiving Emails
metaDescription: Diagnose why Outlook stopped receiving emails by checking Work Offline mode, storage quotas, junk filters, and clearing corrupted local cache files.
intro: A stalled inbox can mean missed client inquiries and delayed multi-factor codes right when you need them most. This guide walks through the most common causes of Outlook not receiving mail, from an accidental offline toggle to storage limits and misconfigured filters, plus how to clear a stuck local cache.

steps.heading: Check your connection status
steps.body: Verify Outlook isn't stuck in Work Offline mode, which blocks new mail from syncing.

steps.heading: Check your storage quota
steps.body: Review your Microsoft 365 cloud storage and mailbox limits, since a full mailbox can cause incoming mail to bounce.

steps.heading: Audit junk and filter settings
steps.body: Check your Junk Email options, blocked senders list, and Focused Inbox filters for anything misrouting real messages.

steps.heading: Inspect your rules
steps.body: Review your Rules dashboard for any accidental redirection scripts that might be diverting incoming mail.

steps.heading: Clear your local cache
steps.body: Purge corrupted local Outlook cache files to resolve deep synchronization freezes.

steps.heading: Run a manual sync and test
steps.body: Force a Send/Receive command, then send yourself a test email to confirm the incoming stream works again.

faq.question: What's the first thing to check if Outlook stops receiving mail?
faq.answer: Start by confirming Outlook isn't stuck in Work Offline mode, since that's one of the most common and easily fixed causes.

faq.question: Could a full mailbox really block incoming mail?
faq.answer: Yes, exceeding your storage quota can cause new messages to bounce instead of arriving in your inbox.

faq.question: Why would a rule cause missing emails?
faq.answer: A misconfigured rule can accidentally redirect or delete incoming messages before you ever see them in your inbox.

faq.question: When should I clear my local Outlook cache?
faq.answer: Clearing your cache is worth trying when sync appears completely frozen and other fixes like checking storage and rules haven't resolved it.
